---
title: "ECON 444 Consulting Project"
author: "Kendall Landwehr & Tatum Barkat"
format: html
---

```{r}
#Reading in data 

install.packages("readxl")

library(readxl)
consumption_data <- read_excel("data/2021-2025_consumption_data.xlsx")

price_data <- read_excel("data/2021-2025_price_data.xlsx")

#Renaming columns
library(dplyr)

consumption_data <- consumption_data %>%
  rename(Hour_DK = `Time(DK)`)
consumption_data <- consumption_data %>%
  rename(Consumption_kWh = `Consumption(kWh)`)
price_data <- price_data %>%
  rename(Spot_Price_DKK = `Spot Price (DKK)`)
price_data <- price_data %>%
  rename(Hour_DK = `Hour (DK)`)

class(consumption_data$Hour_DK)

#Adjusting time zone data
consumption_data$time <- as.POSIXct(consumption_data$Hour_DK,
                                    format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
                                    tz = "Europe/Copenhagen")

price_data$time <- as.POSIXct(price_data$Hour_DK,
                              format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
                              tz = "Europe/Copenhagen")

#Checks
class(consumption_data$time)
attr(consumption_data$time, "tzone")
```

```{r}
#Merging data sets

library(dplyr)

merged_data <- left_join(consumption_data,
                         price_data,
                         by = c("time", "Region"))
#Deleting unnecessary hour columns

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(-Hour_DK.x, -Hour_DK.y)

#Renaming time column and moving to left
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  rename (Time = time)
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(Time, everything())
```

```{r}
# Fit the regression model
model <- lm(Consumption_kWh ~ Spot_Price_DKK, data = merged_data)

# View a summary of results
summary(model)

# Plot residuals vs fitted values
plot(model, which = 1)
```


```{r}
# Normal Q-Q plot to check residuals
plot(model, which = 2)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK, y = Consumption_kWh)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Spot Price (DKK)", y = "Consumption (kWh)",
       title = "Consumption vs Spot Price")
```

```{r}
#Transform the data

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  filter(Spot_Price_DKK > 0, Consumption_kWh > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    log_cons = log(Consumption_kWh),
    log_price = log(Spot_Price_DKK)
  )

```

```{r}
#Control for time
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    hour = as.numeric(format(Time, "%H")),
    weekday = weekdays(Time)
  )

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(month = format(Time, "%m"))
```

```{r}
#Removing some outliers for better clarity
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  filter(
    log_cons < quantile(log_cons, 0.995),
    log_cons > quantile(log_cons, 0.005)
  )
```

```{r}
#Better model after data changes and with other factors added
model_final <- lm(log_cons ~ log_price +
                    factor(hour) +
                    factor(weekday) +
                    factor(month) +
                    factor(Region) +
                    factor(Sector),
                  data = merged_data)

summary(model_final)
```

```{r}
#Adding interaction- "who responds to price"
model_interaction <- lm(log_cons ~ log_price * factor(Sector) +
                         factor(hour) +
                         factor(weekday) +
                         factor(month) +
                         factor(Region),
                       data = merged_data)

summary(model_interaction)
```

```{r}
#Visualization 
sample_data <- merged_data %>% sample_n(5000)

ggplot(sample_data, aes(x = log_price, y = log_cons)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Log Price", y = "Log Consumption",
       title = "Log-Log Relationship (Sampled Data)")
```

```{r}
#Extracting key coefficients for price elasticity

coefs <- coef(summary(model_interaction))

# Baseline (Business)
baseline_elasticity <- coefs["log_price", "Estimate"]

# Private sector
private_elasticity <- baseline_elasticity + 
                      coefs["log_price:factor(Sector)Private", "Estimate"]

# Public sector
public_elasticity <- baseline_elasticity + 
                     coefs["log_price:factor(Sector)Public", "Estimate"]

baseline_elasticity
private_elasticity
public_elasticity


summary(model_interaction)
```

```{r}
# Q-Q plot for final model
plot(model_final, which = 2)

# Extract residuals from final model
residuals_final <- residuals(model_final)

# Create Q-Q plot
ggplot(data.frame(residuals_final), aes(sample = residuals_final)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_qq_line(color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Normal Q-Q Plot of Model Residuals",
    subtitle = "Points deviating from the line indicate departures from normality",
    x = "Theoretical Quantiles (Normal)",
    y = "Observed Residual Quantiles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```


```{r}
# Create elasticity results table
elasticity_data <- data.frame(
  Sector = c("Business", "Private", "Public"),
  Elasticity = c(
    baseline_elasticity,
    private_elasticity,
    public_elasticity
  )
)

ggplot(elasticity_data, aes(x = Sector, y = Elasticity, fill = Sector)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Elasticity, 4)),
            vjust = ifelse(elasticity_data$Elasticity >= 0, -0.5, 1.3),
            size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Business" = "#AEC6CF",
    "Private"  = "#B2E2B2",
    "Public"   = "#F4B6C2"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Estimated Electricity Price Elasticity by Sector",
    x = "Sector",
    y = "Price Elasticity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),

    # ✅ Clean white background (no boxes)
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.background = element_blank(),

    # ✅ Light grid lines only
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray85", size = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```
